summary: Iteration 268: Quick New Client Dialog from Rilievo Editor - All tests passed. Backend: 13/13 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All Playwright UI tests passed. Verified: 1) Quick new client dialog opens from '+' button in rilievo editor, 2) Dialog shows all required fields (Ragione Sociale, Persona Fisica checkbox, Cellulare, Email, Indirizzo, Comune, Provincia, Note), 3) Creating client via dialog saves to /api/clients/ and returns new client with client_id, 4) After creation client is auto-selected in dropdown, 5) Client address propagates to 'Località/Indirizzo' field, 6) Selected client info (address + phone) shows below dropdown, 7) Persona Fisica toggle switches between Cognome/Nome fields and Ragione Sociale, 8) 401 redirect to login page works correctly, 9) POST /api/clients/ creates client with minimal data (no P.IVA, no CF required), 10) New client has notes prefix '[Dati parziali da rilievo]'.
critical_code_review_comments: QuickNewClientDialog.js correctly implements all required fields with proper data-testid attributes
RilievoEditorPage.js correctly integrates QuickNewClientDialog with '+' button next to client select
apiRequest in utils.js correctly handles 401 by redirecting to login page (window.location.href = '/')
Backend POST /api/clients/ correctly accepts minimal data without requiring P.IVA or CF
Frontend correctly auto-selects newly created client and propagates address to location field
Notes field correctly prefixed with '[Dati parziali da rilievo]' to flag incomplete data
Persona Fisica toggle correctly switches between Ragione Sociale and Cognome/Nome fields
seed_data_creation: Created test session 'test_session_268_main' in user_sessions collection for user_97c773827822/ten_1cf1a865bf20
context_for_next_testing_agent: Quick New Client Dialog feature fully implemented and tested. Dialog accessible via '+' button next to client select in /rilievi/new page. Creates client with minimal data, auto-selects in dropdown, propagates address to location field. 401 handling redirects to login page. Session stored in user_sessions collection (not sessions).
